pages.text: Khaya lives in Joza township with Gogo Lucy and Mkhulu Majozi. His mother works far away in Cape Town.

blocks.text: Khaya lives in Joza township with Gogo Lucy and Mkhulu Majozi. His mother works far away in Cape Town.

pages.text: Every morning, Khaya eats porridge with Gogo on the stoep. The kettle whistles on the paraffin stove. The sun peeks over Joza. “Gogo, tell me a story!” Khaya says.

blocks.text: Every morning, Khaya eats porridge with Gogo on the stoep. The kettle whistles on the paraffin stove. The sun peeks over Joza.
“Gogo, tell me a story!”
Khaya says.

pages.text: Gogo smiles. She tells him stories of rivers and dreams. “K haya,” she says, “when you arebigandstrong, youcanbe anythingyouw ”.tna Khaya smiles and nods.

pages.text: One Saturday, Mkhulu switches on the TV. Rowers are gliding across the water. “Mkhulu! What are they doing?” Khaya asks. “They are rowing with oars, my boy,” says Mkhulu.

blocks.text: One Saturday, Mkhulu switches on the TV. Rowers are gliding across the water.
“Mkhulu! What are they doing?” Khaya asks. “They are rowing with oars, my boy,” says Mkhulu.

pages.text: Khaya claps his hands. “Oh wow! I want to row too!” He sings, “I want to row a boat, I want to row a boat!”

blocks.text: Khaya claps his hands. “Oh wow! I want to row too!”
He sings, “I want to row a boat, I want to row a boat!”

pages.text: Mkhulu sings with him, “Khayawantstorow, Khayawantstorow! Rowaboatwithan oar. Row a boatwithanoar.” Gogo laughs and joins in.

blocks.text: Mkhulu sings with him,
“Khayawantstorow, Khayawantstorow!
Rowaboatwithan oar.
Row a boatwithanoar.” Gogo laughs and joins in.

pages.text: From that day, Khaya plays in puddles, draws boats in the sand, and builds toy boats from scraps.

blocks.text: From that day, Khaya plays in puddles, draws boats in the sand, and builds toy boats from scraps.

pages.text: One morning, Mkhulu wakes Khaya early. “Come, Mzukulu, I have a surprise.” Khaya gets ready. Today feels special.

blocks.text: One morning, Mkhulu wakes Khaya early.
“Come, Mzukulu, I have a surprise.”
Khaya gets ready. Today feels special.

pages.text: They t ake a taxi to Port Alfred. Atthe Kowie River, b oats are lined up.

blocks.text: They t ake a taxi to Port Alfred.
Atthe Kowie River, b oats are lined up.

pages.text: Rowers are ready with their oars. “Wow, Mkhulu! This is where people row!” Khaya shouts. He watches boats racing fast across the water. He waves at rowers and takes a photo with one.

blocks.text: Rowers are ready with their oars. “Wow, Mkhulu! This is where people row!” Khaya shouts.
He watches boats racing fast across the water. He waves at rowers and takes a photo with one.

pages.text: At the end of the day, Khaya hugs Mkhulu. “Thank you, Mkhulu. You are the best!” “One day, Khaya,” Mkhulu says, “you will row a boat like a champion.”

blocks.text: At the end of the day, Khaya hugs Mkhulu.
“Thank you, Mkhulu. You are the best!”
“One day, Khaya,” Mkhulu says, “you will row a boat like a champion.”
